summary: The Clinical Research Coordinator role is a strong match for your preference for predictable, standard business hours and a highly structured, procedural work environment. While the role carries high regulatory stress and requires basic clinical math, its emphasis on strict protocol adherence aligns well with your cognitive strengths and avoids the need for fast-paced analytical deduction. This position offers a sustainable long-term career path, provided you can navigate the administrative burden and emotional demands associated with experimental patient care.
